title: Dashboards + alerts for tiered batching metrics (fast/standard/cost-efficient)

Goal
Implement dashboards and alerts for tiered batching defaults (fast / standard / cost-efficient) per the Confluence spec. Primary outcomes:
- Make tier behavior visible (latency, queueing, batching effectiveness, overload mode) broken down by tier/model/region.
- Provide actionable alerting that catches regressi...
text:
Goal
Implement dashboards and alerts for tiered batching defaults (fast / standard / cost-efficient) per the Confluence spec. Primary outcomes:
- Make tier behavior visible (latency, queueing, batching effectiveness, overload mode) broken down by tier/model/region.
- Provide actionable alerting that catches regressions early without paging noise.
- Ensure telemetry/cardinality is controlled and costs stay bounded during rollout.

Scope
- Dashboards:
 - Latency (p50/p95/p99) time series + histograms by tier (fast/standard/cost-efficient), model family, region.
 - Streaming-specific latency panels (TTFT + per-chunk cadence where available) for fast tier.
 - Scheduler health: queue depth, queue wait time, admission/backpressure events, load-shed rate.
 - Batching effectiveness: batch size distribution (prefill/decode), max_batch_tokens utilization, max_wait_ms utilization.
 - Cache: pref...

title: Incidents runbook: overload behavior with tiered batching defaults (fast/standard/cost-efficient)
preview:
## Goal
Provide a single on-call runbook for diagnosing and mitigating overload-related latency regressions when tiered batching defaults are enabled (fast / standard / cost-efficient). This runbook covers:
- symptom → likely cause mapping
- immediate mitigations (no code)
- tier-specific guidance
- rollback / kill-...
text:
## Goal
Provide a single on-call runbook for diagnosing and mitigating overload-related latency regressions when tiered batching defaults are enabled (fast / standard / cost-efficient). This runbook covers:
- symptom → likely cause mapping
- immediate mitigations (no code)
- tier-specific guidance
- rollback / kill-switch procedures
- post-incident follow-ups

This is intended for Runtime + SRE on-call during staged rollout (internal canary → hosted subset → Dedicated opt-in → broader default).

---

## Definitions / mental model
**Overload** here means one (or more) of:
- arrival rate > sustainable throughput for the active tier profile
- queue growth that increases time-to-first-token (TTFT) and/or p99 end-to-end latency
- admission control/load shedding triggers (or fails to trigger)
- scheduler behavior that starves some requests under mixed prompt length / mixed streaming

**Tier...

title: Guardrails for auto-rollback: minimum sample sizes + debounce/holdoff to prevent flapping
preview:
Background
Auto-rollback/pause decisions driven by latency/error SLOs are highly sensitive to short-lived spikes, metric lag, and low-volume routes. We need explicit guardrails so the rollout controller doesn’t flap (pause/rollback/resume repeatedly) or overreact to single-sample anomalies.

This ticket implements a...
text:
Background
Auto-rollback/pause decisions driven by latency/error SLOs are highly sensitive to short-lived spikes, metric lag, and low-volume routes. We need explicit guardrails so the rollout controller doesn’t flap (pause/rollback/resume repeatedly) or overreact to single-sample anomalies.

This ticket implements and documents:
1) Minimum sample size gating for each evaluated SLO window (fast + slow burn).
2) Debounce/holdoff rules (consecutive breach confirmation + cooldown) to avoid oscillation.

Scope
- Add minimum request volume requirements to the SLO evaluator before an SLO window is considered “actionable”.
- Add debounce rules that require breach confirmation across multiple evaluation ticks/windows.
- Add holdoff/cooldown after an action (pause/rollback) so we don’t trigger duplicate actions due to stale evaluations or step-transition jitter.
- Emit clear audit reasons and m...

title: Add drop-rate + delivery latency alerts for audit log pipeline (with on-call routing + runbook links)
preview:
## Context
Audit logging is moving toward 100% coverage for sensitive actions across Hosted/Dedicated/Private. We need alerting that makes audit pipeline failures obvious and actionable (drop rate + excessive delivery latency), with correct paging/routing and clear runbook references.

This ticket is focused on aler...
text:
## Context
Audit logging is moving toward 100% coverage for sensitive actions across Hosted/Dedicated/Private. We need alerting that makes audit pipeline failures obvious and actionable (drop rate + excessive delivery latency), with correct paging/routing and clear runbook references.

This ticket is focused on alert rules + routing + linking to the incident runbook (not on building new pipeline features). It should be compatible with both Hosted and Private deployments, acknowledging that some Private customers forward to their own SIEM and may not run our full managed stack.

## Goals
1) Detect audit event loss (true drops) quickly.
2) Detect sustained high delivery latency (backlog) quickly.
3) Route alerts to the correct on-call (SRE platform) with links to dashboards + runbooks.
4) Avoid alert noise during known failure modes (expected retries, planned maintenance), while still p...

preview:
We’re enabling auto-rollback/pause for rollouts based on SLO breach evaluation. We need production-quality alerting around (a) auto-actions firing, (b) suppression/override usage, and (c) missing/stale monitors that prevent evaluation.

This ticket is owned by Obs/SRE. It covers Grafana/Prometheus alert rules, Alert...
text:
We’re enabling auto-rollback/pause for rollouts based on SLO breach evaluation. We need production-quality alerting around (a) auto-actions firing, (b) suppression/override usage, and (c) missing/stale monitors that prevent evaluation.

This ticket is owned by Obs/SRE. It covers Grafana/Prometheus alert rules, Alertmanager routing, and Slack/oncall destinations. Goal is to ensure the *right* people get notified/paged with enough context to act, without generating paging storms during rollouts.

Primary audiences:
- SRE oncall (platform reliability)
- Service owner oncall (route/model owners) for high-severity rollouts
- Release eng + Console/Platform engineers during staged enablement

Background
Auto-rollback/pause on SLO breach is landing behind a feature flag. Before enabling for any production routes, we need on-call ready documentation, alert routing, and a short training package so responders can (a) trust the automation, (b) quickly validate whether it’s correct, and (c) safely override when needed.

Scope

- Publish/refresh the SRE runbook: https://confluence.redwoodinference.com/display/ENG/Auto-rollback+on+SLO+breach+%28on-call%29

 - Where to find the decision trace + metrics snapshot (Console + audit logs).
 - Triage flow: confirm scope (route/model/region/tier), confirm monitor freshness, confirm sample size gating, check burn-rate windows, validate rollback executor status.
 - Failure modes + what to do:
 - monitor missing/stale (e...

text:
Context
Auto-rollback/pause decisions are now emitted as structured audit events and surfaced in Console. On-call needs a fast, low-noise notification path when an auto action occurs, without creating paging storms (e.g., flapping rollouts, rapid step retries, or multiple regions firing simultaneously).

Non-goals

Run an incident-style game day to validate end-to-end behavior for SLO-driven auto-mitigation during rollouts:

This ticket owns: planning, execution, and capturing concrete follow-ups (owners + tracking).

title: Shadow traffic load testing + overhead benchmarks (validate no impact to user-path SLOs)
preview:
Goal
Validate that enabling shadow traffic does not materially impact the user (control) request path SLOs and that all guardrails behave as designed under load and failure modes.

This ticket covers:
- load generation + benchmark harness
- p50/p95/p99 latency overhead on the control path (chat/generate + streaming)...
text:
Goal
Validate that enabling shadow traffic does not materially impact the user (control) request path SLOs and that all guardrails behave as designed under load and failure modes.

This ticket covers:
- load generation + benchmark harness
- p50/p95/p99 latency overhead on the control path (chat/generate + streaming)
- CPU overhead (router / data plane) and memory growth
- backpressure/circuit breaker behavior when shadow path is slow or down
- telemetry and scoring pipeline saturation safety checks (shadow path must degrade without impacting user path)

Why
Shadowing introduces extra work in the router (fingerprinting/bucketing, enqueue/mirror, metadata, partial capture for streaming). We must prove the user path remains within SLO and overhead budgets, and document a go/no-go gate for beta enablement.

Success criteria / acceptance
1) Control-path latency overhead with shadowing enab...

SRE/on-call has dashboards, but links aren’t consistently discoverable from runbo...
text:
## Context
Support escalations (SUP tickets + Slack bridges) routinely stall on the same questions:
- “Is this a region issue vs customer-specific?”
- “Are we seeing elevated 5xx/429s?”
- “Is streaming unhealthy (disconnects/resets)?”

SRE/on-call has dashboards, but links aren’t consistently discoverable from runbooks, and many views aren’t appropriate for Support to access directly (too much internal detail, noisy, or requires elevated permissions). This ticket creates:
1) canonical dashboard links referenced from runbooks/templates, and
2) a Support-safe “Quick View” set of dashboards (aggregate only) that Support can use during triage and while writing customer updates.

This work is part of the Feb 2026 escalation playbook refresh and known-issues sprint, where we’re standardizing required context for pages and reducing time-to-triage.

## Goals
- Provide a single, canonical set ...

title: Dedicated autoscaling stability guardrails (cooldowns + min capacity enforcement) to prevent oscillation
preview:
## Context
Dedicated GPU pools have shown autoscaling oscillation (“churn”) under spiky token-rate workloads and when queue depth signals flap. This manifests as latency spikes (p95/p99) and occasional elevated error rates when scale-down happens too aggressively and warm-up costs dominate.

This is a top escalation...
text:
## Context
Dedicated GPU pools have shown autoscaling oscillation (“churn”) under spiky token-rate workloads and when queue depth signals flap. This manifests as latency spikes (p95/p99) and occasional elevated error rates when scale-down happens too aggressively and warm-up costs dominate.

This is a top escalation driver for Dedicated customers and is explicitly referenced in Support’s Known Issues Sprint.

**Primary escalation:** SUP-1844 (Dedicated autoscaling oscillation causing latency)

## Problem statement
Current autoscaling policies can:
- scale down quickly after a short dip in load, then scale back up within minutes (repeated cycles)
- allow effective capacity to drop below a customer’s “safe minimum” during transient dips
- cause repeated cold starts / model warm-up that increase tail latency and amplify paging noise

## Goals
1) Add guardrails to reduce oscillation witho...

title: Add metrics + dashboard + alerts for offline bundle signing pipeline health
preview:
Context
Offline bundle signing is now a release gating requirement for Redwood Private. We have had intermittent CI issues in the signing stage (timeouts/HSM emulator instability in staging; occasional retries masking real regressions). We also need better early warning for key-rotation readiness (e.g., impending ce...
text:
Context
Offline bundle signing is now a release gating requirement for Redwood Private. We have had intermittent CI issues in the signing stage (timeouts/HSM emulator instability in staging; occasional retries masking real regressions). We also need better early warning for key-rotation readiness (e.g., impending cert expiry / trust-store drift) so air-gapped customers are not surprised.

Goal
Provide end-to-end visibility into the signing pipeline so Release Eng + SRE can answer:
- Is signing healthy right now?
- Are signed bundles being produced within expected latency?
- What are the top failure causes (HSM/KMS, canonicalization, upload/publish, verification tests)?
- Are we at risk for a key rotation event (expiring signing cert, trust store not updated, installers not compatible)?

Non-goals
- Re-architect the signing pipeline (tracked elsewhere).
- Build customer-facing telemetr...

title: Alerting for quality regressions + scoring signal lag (shadow traffic)
preview:
Problem
- We are adding “shadow traffic” quality scoring during rollouts. Beta customers + internal dogfood need confidence that:
 (1) we will detect statistically meaningful quality regressions in candidate variants
 (2) we will detect when the scoring pipeline is missing/lagging (so dashboards don’t silently go st...
text:
Problem
- We are adding “shadow traffic” quality scoring during rollouts. Beta customers + internal dogfood need confidence that:
 (1) we will detect statistically meaningful quality regressions in candidate variants
 (2) we will detect when the scoring pipeline is missing/lagging (so dashboards don’t silently go stale)
 (3) we will do this without blowing up cardinality or paging on noise.

Scope
- Define, implement, and validate alert rules for:
 A) Quality regression detection (control vs shadow variant deltas)
 B) Missing/lagging scoring signals (ingestion lag, queue backlog, scoring worker errors)
 C) “Bad data” conditions that make quality deltas untrustworthy (insufficient samples, high drop rate, shadow-target errors)

Non-goals
- Auto-rollback integration (tracked separately). Alerts should feed rollout decisions but not execute them in this ticket.
- Per-prompt / per-custome...

Shadow traffic introduces a new set of control-plane and Console actions (creating/updating Shadow Experiments, attaching shadow policies to routes/rollouts, tuning sampling, targets, and retention/redaction knobs). For Dedicated/Private and regulated customers, we need a complete audit trail of who chang...
text:
Background
Shadow traffic introduces a new set of control-plane and Console actions (creating/updating Shadow Experiments, attaching shadow policies to routes/rollouts, tuning sampling, targets, and retention/redaction knobs). For Dedicated/Private and regulated customers, we need a complete audit trail of who changed what, when, and from where.

This ticket adds audit log entries for:
1) Shadow Experiment lifecycle actions (Console backend)
2) Shadow policy lifecycle + attach/detach changes (control plane / route config)
3) Permission model updates so the right roles can manage these settings and the right roles can view the audit trail

Goals
- Ensure every security-relevant mutation is written to the audit log service with consistent event naming.
- Provide “before/after” change context that is useful for investigations without storing prompts/PII or high-cardinality payloads.
- En...

title: Backpressure + circuit breaking for shadow traffic (fail-open + graceful degradation)
preview:
Context
Shadow traffic must be strictly fail-open: if shadow target or scoring/ingestion is unhealthy, the user (control) request path must remain unaffected (latency/throughput/errors). During dogfood we saw scoring queue lag (see Slack incident thread: sources/slack/incidents/1768180020-shadow-scoring-queue-backlo...
text:
Context
Shadow traffic must be strictly fail-open: if shadow target or scoring/ingestion is unhealthy, the user (control) request path must remain unaffected (latency/throughput/errors). During dogfood we saw scoring queue lag (see Slack incident thread: sources/slack/incidents/1768180020-shadow-scoring-queue-backlog.json). We need explicit backpressure and circuit breaking across (1) routing mirroring, (2) telemetry ingestion, and (3) scoring pipeline so we degrade gracefully instead of amplifying load or causing cardinality/infra alerts.

Problem statement
Today the shadow path will continue attempting mirrors and producing telemetry even when downstream components (shadow target, ingestion, scoring) are saturated. This can:
- increase CPU/network overhead on the data plane
- increase metrics/telemetry load when pipelines are already behind
- create delayed/incorrect dashboards (sta...

title: Regional failover automation upgrade (Hosted API + Dedicated) — umbrella
preview:
## Context
Today, regional failover is a mix of manual actions and partially automated routing policies. Detection can be noisy (false positives), real regional impairment can be slow to identify, and end-to-end safety validation is hard.

This umbrella tracks the Reliability Program work to:
- Improve trigger signa...
text:
## Context
Today, regional failover is a mix of manual actions and partially automated routing policies. Detection can be noisy (false positives), real regional impairment can be slow to identify, and end-to-end safety validation is hard.

This umbrella tracks the Reliability Program work to:
- Improve trigger signal pipeline (reachability, error burn, tail latency burn, capacity headroom, network symptoms)
- Add guardrails + deterministic policy evaluation (avoid flapping/oscillation)
- Validate via controlled failover tests (“game days”) with measurable SLO outcomes
- Improve observability so on-call can quickly distinguish region impairment vs local service issues

## Goals (success criteria)
- Detection + failover time reduced vs baseline (target: automated recommendation within minutes; execution gated by safety limits/optional approval hooks)
- Fewer false-positive regional trig...
tags: Reliability Program — Regional Failover Automation Upgrade

preview:

text:
Goal
Finalize numeric latency targets for the three customer-facing SLO tiers (fast / standard / cost-efficient) and codify pass/fail gates used by the perf suite + perf-canary so we can safely roll out workload-aware batching defaults.

Scope
- Provide concrete numeric targets (p50/p95/p99) per tier for:
 - Non-streaming requests (end-to-end latency)
 - Streaming requests (TTFT and per-token cadence; explicitly call out what we gate on)
- Define acceptance gates for:
 - Benchmark-lab suite runs (pre-merge / nightly)
 - Perf-canary (merge blocker) thresholds and baseline refresh policy
- Specify which model families/hardware/regions are in-scope for gating for runtime-1.22 launch.

Non-goals
- We are not defining customer contractual SLAs here (Product will reference these for messaging, but SRE owns SLA docs).
- We are not choosing batching knob values here (that’s the scheduler/prof...

text:
Purpose
- Final SRE sign-off on guardrails before enabling the short/long prompt-aware scheduler in production (limited canary in us-east, subset of Dedicated pools).
- Define concrete rollback triggers and “stop the line” conditions.
- Confirm dashboards/alerts and on-call coverage for ramp window.

Context
- Mixed workloads (short chat prompts interleaved with long-context prompts) cause head-of-line blocking under current continuous batching selection, inflating user-visible p95.
- The prototype introduces class-based scheduling (short/long) with aging/starvation prevention and per-iteration token budgets.

text:
We need first-class dependency health visibility for KMS across Hosted/Dedicated/Private so on-call can quickly answer:
- Are we failing because KMS is down / throttling / permission misconfigured?
- Is latency creeping up enough to trip request SLOs or trigger retry amplification?

This ticket ships:

2) Alerts with sane defaults and environment-aware routing (page only for prod; notify-only for staging).
3) Threshold tuning pass to reduce noise during rollout (see eng-sre thread about noisy latency alerts).

Dashboard and alerting should align with the standardized metric names/tags introduced by the KMS wrapper work (ENG-4822/4823) and be linked from the decrypt failures runbook.

title: Launch-week monitoring + rate-limit guardrails for public benchmark landing page
preview:
Context
The benchmark report launch is expected to drive bursty traffic to the public landing page and interactive table (JSON results feed). We need launch-week observability, alerting, and guardrails (rate limits + cache protections) to avoid: (a) marketing site latency/timeouts, (b) cache miss storms, (c) runaway...
text:
Context
The benchmark report launch is expected to drive bursty traffic to the public landing page and interactive table (JSON results feed). We need launch-week observability, alerting, and guardrails (rate limits + cache protections) to avoid: (a) marketing site latency/timeouts, (b) cache miss storms, (c) runaway origin load on the benchmark results service, (d) elevated 429s/5xx that degrade the public narrative.

Goals
- Ship a launch dashboard for real-time monitoring (site + results feed + CDN).
- Add pragmatic guardrails: rate limits, circuit breakers, and caching defaults that survive traffic spikes.
- Define an on-call / escalation path + runbook for launch day.
- Validate via pre-launch load test and a controlled cache-warm workflow.

Non-goals
- Re-architect the results service (keep changes minimal; we can iterate after launch).
- “Perfect” DDoS protection beyond existing...

title: Burst pools: implement global fleet safety cap + emergency circuit breaker
preview:
Context
We’re introducing Dedicated “burst capacity” via shared Redwood-managed burst pools per region/hardware class. We need a hard, global fleet safety cap + emergency circuit breaker so a burst surge (or a bug/telemetry lag) cannot degrade non-burst tenants or platform-wide SLOs.

This ticket is specifically abo...
text:
Context
We’re introducing Dedicated “burst capacity” via shared Redwood-managed burst pools per region/hardware class. We need a hard, global fleet safety cap + emergency circuit breaker so a burst surge (or a bug/telemetry lag) cannot degrade non-burst tenants or platform-wide SLOs.

This ticket is specifically about: (1) a global cap that limits burst admissions at the pool/fleet level, and (2) a circuit breaker mechanism that can rapidly deny further burst admissions (and optionally shed burst load) when we detect neighbor impact or unsafe saturation.

Problem statement
During beta we observed two failure modes:
- Pool saturation can cascade into tail amplification for nearby workloads (esp. when bursts coincide with long-context spikes).
- Telemetry lag can make admission decisions “optimistic” for a short period, causing oversubscription beyond safe margins.

We need an explicit,...

title: Support playbook + standardized denial reason codes for Dedicated Burst admission
preview:
Problem
Support/CS are seeing multiple flavors of "burst denied" behavior during beta (429s, overload, routing fallback, and occasionally silent slowdown). Without standardized denial reason codes and a consistent customer-facing explanation, Support cannot reliably answer:
- Why was my burst denied?
- Is this expec...
text:
Problem
Support/CS are seeing multiple flavors of "burst denied" behavior during beta (429s, overload, routing fallback, and occasionally silent slowdown). Without standardized denial reason codes and a consistent customer-facing explanation, Support cannot reliably answer:
- Why was my burst denied?
- Is this expected (limits/guardrails) vs. an incident?
- What should we do next (retry/backoff vs. request config change vs. escalate to SRE/Fleet)?

This ticket standardizes the denial reason codes emitted by the Burst Admission Controller and ensures:
1) The reason codes are surfaced consistently through the gateway/routing path.
2) Support tooling exists to explain “why” for a specific request/tenant/time window.
3) A Support playbook exists with customer-ready language, troubleshooting steps, and escalation paths.

Scope
A. Denial reason code taxonomy (v1)
- Define canonical machine ...

title: SIEM audit export: E2E integration tests (Splunk/Datadog/S3) + chaos scenarios + idempotency assertions
preview: Build and wire up end-to-end integration tests for audit export adapters (Splunk HEC, Datadog Logs intake, S3 delivery) using sandbox/stubbed endpoints, and validate delivery semantics (at-least-once), dedup behavior (event_id), checkpointing, backfill windows, and failure handling (retry/backoff, DLQ). Include chao...
text:
Build and wire up end-to-end integration tests for audit export adapters (Splunk HEC, Datadog Logs intake, S3 delivery) using sandbox/stubbed endpoints, and validate delivery semantics (at-least-once), dedup behavior (event_id), checkpointing, backfill windows, and failure handling (retry/backoff, DLQ). Include chaos scenarios to ensure exporter remains stable during downstream throttling/outages and does not violate max-lag safety controls.

Context:
- We explicitly guarantee at-least-once delivery (not exactly-once). E2E tests must prove: (1) exporter can safely retry without dropping events, (2) replay/backfill produces duplicates as expected, and (3) duplicates are detectable via stable event_id for downstream dedup.
- Beta customers are already asking for “missing events” explanations; we need hard, reproducible tests to back runbook guidance and reduce regressions during rapid a...

title: Auto-stop and rollback canary rollouts on SLO breach (5xx/timeouts/p95 latency)

text:
Implement automatic stop conditions for Hosted canary rollouts and wire them into the rollout orchestrator.

Scope:
- Evaluate SLO regressions comparing baseline vs canary cohorts:
 - 5xx error rate
 - timeout rate
 - p95 latency regression
- When stop condition trips, orchestrator should either:
 - auto-pause (default for Beta), or
 - auto-rollback (GA default for tenants with guardrail policy enabled), depending on tenant policy + rollout configuration.
- Notify: Console surface "Auto-stopped" reason + event timeline entry; send internal notification (PagerDuty for severe, Slack for non-severe) + optional customer email/webhook (if enabled for tenant).

Non-goals:
- Automated quality gates as a hard blocker (still allow optional manual quality metrics display; no automated gating in this ticket).

Notes:
- Must be safe under partial telemetry outages: avoid silent failures; avoid ro...

text:
Ship the initial dashboards and alert rules for prefix/KV cache telemetry so SRE + Runtime can detect regressions (hit-rate drops, eviction pressure spikes, wasted prefill spikes) and differentiate real cache changes from telemetry pipeline failures.

Primary audiences:
- SRE oncall (triage + mitigation)
- Runtime oncall (miss reasons, eviction/pressure correlation)
- Platform/Telemetry (ingestion + label drops)

Dashboards must support operational breakdowns without cardinality blowups: model, route (allowlisted), deployment mode, region, and request-shape buckets.

text:
Goal
Ship the internal Unit Economics Dashboard v1 safely with explicit quality gates, SLO readiness, access controls, and a phased rollout plan aligned with Finance signoff.

Scope (this ticket)
- Consolidate the v1 launch checklist into a single source of truth for go/no-go.
- Define the phased rollout (dogfood -> Finance/Product -> broader Eng -> exec KPI consumption) including comms and support expectations.
- Define launch gates that are objectively verifiable (queries, dashboards, alerts, reconciliation report).

Non-goals
- Building remaining pipeline/UI work (tracked in dependencies).

title: E2E test suite for Hosted data residency region pinning (blocking, streaming, fallback)
preview:
## Context
We’re rolling out Hosted data residency enforcement (region pinning + deterministic request-time blocks) for Enterprise customers. Support and Sales escalations show the highest-risk regressions are:
- Region-pinned orgs accidentally routed to a disallowed region via endpoint selection or fallback.
- Stre...
text:
## Context
We’re rolling out Hosted data residency enforcement (region pinning + deterministic request-time blocks) for Enterprise customers. Support and Sales escalations show the highest-risk regressions are:
- Region-pinned orgs accidentally routed to a disallowed region via endpoint selection or fallback.
- Streaming endpoints returning generic 500 / truncated frames instead of the standardized residency violation error.
- Cross-region fallback attempts when a region is degraded/capacity constrained.

This ticket adds an end-to-end test suite that runs against staging (and optionally prod-canary) to continuously validate residency enforcement behavior across routing + gateway + streaming.

## Goals
- Catch regressions in the full request path (gateway → policy eval → routing → response formatting).
- Validate error contract for blocked requests is consistent across streaming and n...
tags: Enterprise Security & Compliance Foundation — Hosted data residency enforcement

title: Oncall runbook + playbook docs for Unit Economics dashboard/pipeline (link from alerts)
preview:
## Context
We’re rolling out the internal Unit Economics dashboard and hourly pipeline. For exec/Finance reporting, we need predictable operational response when freshness/completeness or reconciliation drifts. Alerts are being added (see pipeline SLO + completeness checks), but oncall currently lacks a single, deci...
text:
## Context
We’re rolling out the internal Unit Economics dashboard and hourly pipeline. For exec/Finance reporting, we need predictable operational response when freshness/completeness or reconciliation drifts. Alerts are being added (see pipeline SLO + completeness checks), but oncall currently lacks a single, decision-tree style playbook.

This ticket creates the oncall runbook pages and ensures *every* pipeline/dashboard alert includes a direct link to the relevant section.

## Goals
- Provide a single “start here” runbook for Unit Economics pipeline + dashboard discrepancies.
- Add decision trees for the most common failure modes: missing hours, output token undercount (esp streaming), missing dimensions (region/tier/model/quant_profile), cost attribution/rate-card issues, and reconciliation mismatches vs billing.
- Ensure alert payloads link to the runbook and the exact remediati...

title: Automate periodic restore drill in staging for Redwood Private and report results to Slack/on-call
preview:
Goal
Automate a repeatable restore drill for a Redwood Private staging environment, on a fixed cadence, using the same supported backup artifacts + restore workflow customers will use. Report drill outcomes (success/failure + key timings) to Slack and wire alerting consistent with SRE paging policy.

Why
We’ve been ...
text:
Goal
Automate a repeatable restore drill for a Redwood Private staging environment, on a fixed cadence, using the same supported backup artifacts + restore workflow customers will use. Report drill outcomes (success/failure + key timings) to Slack and wire alerting consistent with SRE paging policy.

Why
We’ve been doing ad-hoc restore tests and they keep drifting from “customer-realistic” procedures. We need a predictable signal that:
1) backups are usable,
2) restore remains deterministic across platform versions,
3) validation checks catch the most common restore regressions (ordering, missing CRDs, bad Helm values, auth/routing config not restored).

Scope
In scope:
- A scheduled restore drill (weekly by default) in staging.
- Uses the Private installer restore workflow (restore apply) against the most recent valid control-plane backup artifact.
- Runs post-restore validation (smo...

title: Execute Private DR game day + write-up and follow-up action plan
preview:
Goal
- Run a realistic disaster recovery (DR) game day for Redwood Private (VPC and on-prem assumptions), validate backup/restore runbooks + automation, and produce an actionable follow-up plan.

Why
- Enterprise prospects (FinBank, MedData) are explicitly asking for evidence of repeatable DR procedures and RPO/RTO ...
text:
Goal
- Run a realistic disaster recovery (DR) game day for Redwood Private (VPC and on-prem assumptions), validate backup/restore runbooks + automation, and produce an actionable follow-up plan.

Why
- Enterprise prospects (FinBank, MedData) are explicitly asking for evidence of repeatable DR procedures and RPO/RTO attainment. This game day is the internal bar for whether we can confidently claim “supported + documented + repeatable” recovery.

Scope for this game day
- Primary: Control-plane restore into a clean Kubernetes cluster using the new installer workflows (backup create / restore apply) + documented runbooks.
- Secondary: Validate observability and on-call readiness (dashboards/alerts, failure modes, escalation criteria).
- Out of scope: Full etcd cluster restore as the primary path (covered by infra runbook as last resort), and data-plane “stateful” restoration (data-plane ...

title: Instrument canonical per-deployment throughput metrics (deployment_id/model/region) for Dedicated SLO
preview:
Context / why
We are formalizing a Dedicated throughput SLO and need a single canonical throughput signal that is attributable to a specific Dedicated deployment. Today we have a mix of per-pod / per-cluster perf counters and some request logs, but nothing that can be used as a stable SLO input for: (a) per-customer...
text:
Context / why
We are formalizing a Dedicated throughput SLO and need a single canonical throughput signal that is attributable to a specific Dedicated deployment. Today we have a mix of per-pod / per-cluster perf counters and some request logs, but nothing that can be used as a stable SLO input for: (a) per-customer reporting (deployment_id), (b) tier-aware dashboards, and (c) SLO burn-rate alerts. This ticket adds per-deployment throughput metrics with consistent token accounting semantics (esp. streaming) and the required labels.

Scope
- Emit canonical throughput “inputs” at the inference gateway / request path for all Dedicated deployments:
 - tokens counted (input + output) with explicit definitions
 - wall clock generation time used for throughput calculations
 - request counts by completion outcome
- All metrics MUST be tagged by:
 - deployment_id (Dedicated deployment identifi...
